subject: Databases
title: What Is Normalization
description: A quick, practical guide to What Is Normalization.

hook.text: An orders table repeats the customer name and address in every row. Change the address? Update hundreds of rows.

buildup.text: Normalization organizes data to reduce redundancy. Each fact is stored in exactly one place.

discovery.text: Move customer data to a Customers table. Orders reference customer_id. One update fixes the address everywhere.

twist.text: Normalization adds joins. More tables means more complex queries. It's a trade-off, not a free win.

climax.text: Most production databases are normalized to some degree. Pure denormalization leads to data chaos.

punchline.text: Normalize to eliminate redundancy. Each fact, one place.

quiz.question: What is the primary goal of normalization?
options: Reducing data redundancy by storing each fact in exactly one place
Making the database use less disk space
Eliminating the need for SQL queries
